sections.title: Welcome to Walking
sections.description:
This SmartUrbanity questionnaire focuses on your experience as a pedestrian. Your answers help us understand the conditions and barriers people face when walking in the city, and identify opportunities to make walking safer, more comfortable, and more enjoyable. Participation is voluntary and anonymous.

https://www.smarturbanity.com/about

sections.title: Walking Habits
sections.description: Questions about how and when you walk as part of your daily travel.

questions.text: Do you walk as a primary mode of travel at least once a week? (This means walking as the main way to complete a trip, not just short transfers between other modes.)

options.text: Yes
No

questions.text: Which of the following physical barriers do you face on your walking route? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Steps or steep curbs without ramps
Missing or broken dropped kerbs at crossings
Paths too narrow for a wheelchair, stroller, or two people to pass
Uneven paving, holes, or soft ground (grass or gravel)
Parked cars, trash bins, or construction signs blocking the path
Overhanging branches or bushes narrowing the usable space
Missing or broken tactile paving (for visually impaired users)
I don't face any physical barrier
Other

questions.text: How do poor sidewalk conditions (e.g., holes, uneven surfaces, or crumbling edges) affect your journey?

options.text: It does not affect my trip at all
I notice it but it does not significantly change my behaviour or comfort
I continue on the same path but it makes me feel uncomfortable, stressed, or more tired
I swerve or switch to the other side of the road to avoid bad sections
I completely avoid streets with bad pavement, even if the trip takes longer

questions.text: How do temporary obstacles (e.g., roadworks, bins, or parked scooters) typically affect your walking route?

options.text: I rarely encounter obstacles that affect my movement
I occasionally need to walk around obstacles, but they do not significantly affect my journey
Obstacles sometimes slow me down or make walking less comfortable
Obstacles frequently force me to change my path or walk closer to traffic
Obstacles regularly make the route difficult, stressful, or inaccessible

questions.text: To what extent does the steepness of your route affect your walking?

options.text: Not at all
Slightly
Moderately
Significantly
Extremely

questions.text: How do the quality and availability of pedestrian crossings (e.g., zebra crossings, pedestrian signals, or crossing islands) affect your journey?

options.text: Crossings are well placed and I feel safe using them
Crossings exist but I often feel rushed or uncertain while using them
Crossings are too infrequent and I sometimes have to cross in unsafe places
The poor quality or lack of crossings forces me to significantly change my route
I avoid certain roads entirely because I cannot cross them safely on foot

questions.text: How does the availability of benches and rest areas affect your ability to complete your journey?

options.text: Seating has no influence on my travel choices or physical stamina
I notice the lack of seating makes the trip less comfortable but it does not affect my ability to finish
I do not plan my trip around them but I use them if I feel unexpectedly tired or am carrying heavy items
I specifically choose routes with seating so I can take breaks, even if it is not the most direct path
I rely on rest areas to make my journey possible; without them I could not travel this far

questions.text: How does the quality of street lighting affect your choice of route or your decision to walk after dark?

options.text: Lighting levels have no influence on my travel choices or comfort
I notice poor lighting but it does not significantly change how or when I travel
I still use the route but I move faster, avoid certain spots, or stay on high alert
I take a much longer alternative route specifically to stay in better-lit areas
I do not walk after dark because of poor lighting

questions.text: How does the presence (or lack) of trees, parks, or green spaces affect your route choice?

options.text: They do not influence my route choice at all
I notice greenery, but it rarely affects the routes I choose
Green spaces sometimes influence the routes I choose
I often prefer routes with trees, parks, or green spaces
I actively choose routes because they are greener or more pleasant

questions.text: How does shade and heat (e.g., from sun exposure or hot pavement) affect your walking journeys during warmer months?

options.text: Heat and sun have no effect on my walking journeys
I feel uncomfortable in the heat but I continue as planned
I adjust my departure time or choose shadier routes when possible
I significantly shorten or avoid walking trips during hot weather
I stop walking outdoors entirely during the hottest periods

questions.text: How does the quality of signs and wayfinding on your route affect your confidence as a pedestrian?

options.text: I never look at signs; their quality has no effect on my navigation
I find signs helpful if I need them but I rarely feel lost
I rely on signs to confirm I am on the right track or to find exits and crossings
I often have to stop and double-check my position because signs are unclear or missing
Signs are so poor that I cannot navigate without using a GPS or phone app

questions.text: How do traffic levels and congestion on your route typically affect your walking experience?

options.text: The route is generally quiet or traffic has no influence on my walking experience
I notice the traffic but feel physically separated and safe from it
The traffic is frustrating but does not significantly affect my stress levels or route
Traffic makes me feel constantly anxious or unsafe during my walk
I choose much longer routes or change my travel times specifically to avoid heavy traffic

questions.text: Which categories of road users most negatively affect your sense of safety while walking? (Select up to 3 options)

options.text: Car drivers
Motorcycle or scooter riders
Bus drivers
Delivery vehicles (vans, cargo bikes, scooters)
Taxi or ride-hailing drivers
Cyclists
E-scooter riders
Other pedestrians
None — no category of road user negatively affects my feeling of safety
Other

questions.text: How do cyclists and e-scooter riders affect your sense of safety while walking?

options.text: They do not affect my sense of safety while walking
I notice them, but I rarely feel unsafe or uncomfortable
I sometimes feel the need to pay extra attention or move aside
I often feel uncomfortable or unsafe when cyclists or e-scooters pass close to me
Encounters with cyclists or e-scooters frequently make walking stressful or feel dangerous

questions.text: How does walking alongside or crossing high-speed roads (e.g., main roads or highways) affect your sense of safety?

options.text: It does not affect my sense of safety
I feel slightly uncomfortable but manage
I feel unsafe and try to minimise time spent near high-speed roads
I take significantly longer routes to avoid walking alongside high-speed roads
I avoid trips that require me to walk alongside or cross high-speed roads

questions.text: Does walking as part of your daily travel contribute positively to your physical or mental health?

options.text: Not at all, I walk out of necessity, not choice
A little, I appreciate it but it is not significant
Moderately, it contributes to my overall activity level
Quite a lot, it is an important part of my daily physical activity
Very much, walking is a key part of my physical and mental wellbeing

questions.text: What improvements would most encourage you to walk more often or for longer distances? (Select up to 3 options)

options.text: Better sidewalk conditions and maintenance
More shade, trees, or green spaces along routes
Better street lighting
Safer crossings and reduced vehicle speeds
More benches and rest areas
Cleaner and better-maintained public spaces
Better separation from cyclists and e-scooters
Clearer wayfinding signs and maps
Nothing — I am satisfied with current conditions
Other

it.#User_position: Mostra la posizione del browser sulla mappa
it.#infoContent:
Istruzioni

Autenticazione

L'utente deve autenticarsi tramite un suo account Google, oppure registrandosi tramite email e password.

Aggiungere

Per aggiungre una segnalazione, cliccare sul relativo tasto nella barra in alto.
Si apre un form in cui bisogna:
a) scegliere il tema dal menù a tendina;
b) indicare il luogo, che può essere quello dalla propria posizione (tasto Attuale), oppure un punto sulla mappa (tasto Clicca), oppure un indirizzo in formato testuale che deve però essere validato (tasto Cerca);
c) descrivere in modo succinto il problema nella casella Testo;
d) selezionare (opzionale) una immagine (tasto Foto);
e) riempire altri eventuali campi richiesti (che potrebbero trovarsi scorrendo lo schermo verso il basso).
Infine, inviare la segnalazione cliccando sul tasto Salva in alto.

Consultare

Per consultare una segnalazione, cliccare il relativo segnaposto sulla mappa. Comparirà un popup con le relative informazioni.

Modificare

Solo l'autore e gli amministratori del sistema possono modificare una segnalazione.
Al momento è possibile solo eliminare e chiudere (tasto Risolto) una segnalazione.

Filtrare

Per visualizzare un sotto insieme delle segnalazioni, cliccare sul tasto Filtra nella barra in alto.
Si apre un form in cui bisogna:
a) scegliere la data iniziale e finale dell'intervallo di validità;
b) scegliere la zona di interesse;
c) scegliere gli stati di interesse (aperte, risolte, eliminate; nuove, presentate);
Le segnalazioni non selezionate appaiono in trasparenza.
d) selezionare i temi dal bottone in alto a destra.
I temi non selezionati vengono nascosti.

Stampare

Cliccando sul tasto Stampa viene scaricato un file pdf con le sole segnalazioni selezionate.

Presentare

La stampa viene memorizzata nel sistema e può essere in seguito selezionata dal menù a tendina al fine di presentatare le segnalazioni ivi contenute cliccando sul relativo tasto a fianco.

La mappa

Per ritornare alla mappa, cliccare il relativo tasto nella barra in alto.
E' possibile sceglere tra diverse mappe di sfondo dal bottone in alto a destra.

it.#This survey is part of an extended rese: Questo questionario fa parte di un più ampio progetto di ricerca che combina più questionari e dati GPS (per chi utilizza l’app). Insieme, questi strumenti ci aiutano a progettare spazi urbani più sicuri e inclusivi.
it.#You may optionally provide your email a: Puoi, se lo desideri, fornire il tuo indirizzo email per generare un codice di partecipazione anonimo. Questo ci permette di collegare le tue risposte tra diversi questionari per analisi longitudinali, senza mai memorizzare la tua email reale.
it.#To learn more about how we collect and: Per saperne di più su come raccogliamo e proteggiamo i dati, leggi la nostra pagina di trasparenza e protezione dei dati.
it.#I have read and agree to the privacy no: Ho letto e accetto l’informativa sulla privacy che descrive come i miei dati vengono raccolti e utilizzati a fini di ricerca.
it.#Would you like to provide your email ad: Vuoi fornire il tuo indirizzo email per generare un codice di partecipazione anonimo? (facoltativo)
it.#Add a note if this question needs revie: Aggiungi una nota se questa domanda necessita di revisione o correzione.
La tua risposta è stata registrata.
it.#Please confirm your consent to data pro: Per favore conferma il tuo consenso al trattamento dati prima di continuare.
it.#create a new mobility profile: crea un nuovo profilo di mobilità
it.#show mobility profile description: mostra descrizione del profilo di mobilità
it.#mobility_attitudes: Come ti senti rispetto alla mobilità

fa.#This survey is part of an extended rese: این پرسشنامه بخشی از یک پروژه پژوهشی گسترده‌تر است که چندین پرسشنامه و داده‌های موقعیت‌یاب GPS (برای افرادی که از برنامه استفاده می‌کنند) را ترکیب می‌کند. این اطلاعات به ما کمک می‌کند تا فضاهای شهری ایمن‌تر و فراگیرتری طراحی کنیم.
fa.#You may optionally provide your email a: شما می‌توانید به صورت اختیاری نشانی ایمیل خود را وارد کنید تا یک کد ناشناس شرکت در پژوهش تولید شود. این کار به ما امکان می‌دهد پاسخ‌های شما را در پرسشنامه‌های مختلف برای تحلیل‌های طولی به هم پیوند دهیم، بدون آنکه ایمیل واقعی شما ذخیره شود.
fa.#To learn more about how we collect and: برای آگاهی بیشتر درباره نحوه جمع‌آوری و حفاظت از داده‌ها، مرور کلی شفافیت و حفاظت از داده‌های ما را بخوانید.
fa.#I have read and agree to the privacy no: من اطلاعیه حریم خصوصی را خوانده‌ام و با نحوه جمع‌آوری و استفاده از داده‌هایم برای اهداف پژوهشی موافقم.
fa.#Would you like to provide your email ad: آیا مایل هستید برای ایجاد یک کد مشارکت ناشناس، آدرس ایمیل خود را وارد کنید؟ (اختیاری)
fa.#Add a note if this question needs revie: در صورتی که این سؤال نیاز به بازبینی یا اصلاح دارد، یادداشتی اضافه کنید.
